name: Bahrain
summary: Day 96 (Wednesday June 3; covers 2 June afternoon -> 3 June morning). **BAHRAIN WAS DIRECTLY TARGETED IN THE 3 JUNE BARRAGE — ITS DEFENCE FORCE INTERCEPTED 3 IRANIAN MISSILES AND A NUMBER OF DRONES AIMED AT 'CIVILIAN SITES,' NO CASUALTIES; THE IRGC FALSELY CLAIMED IT HIT THE US FIFTH FLEET HQ.** Bahrain's Defence Force General Command said it intercepted three Iranian missiles and several drones aimed at 'civilian sites,' with no casualties reported. The IRGC claimed it had struck 'the headquarters of the US Fifth Fleet in Bahrain' (Manama) — CENTCOM explicitly rejected the claim as FALSE, stating all Iranian attacks on US forces failed and no US personnel or assets were harmed (intel B3; a clean 'one-side's-framing-as-fact' case). Bahrain — which hosts the US Fifth Fleet — was thus a direct interception party in the cycle's first casualty-producing barrage, but absorbed the strikes with no damage/casualties (the lethal damage landed on Kuwait's civilian airport). Watch whether Bahrain escalates its rhetoric toward Iran after being targeted on its own soil (a possible follow to Kuwait's diplomat expulsion), or holds a quieter posture. **No Bahrain trigger moves to active — the strikes were intercepted, zero-casualty; the Fifth-Fleet-HQ hit is a CENTCOM-denied Iranian CLAIM.** PRIOR — Day 73-74 (Mon-Tue May 11-12). **A5 BAHRAIN 41 IRGC-LINKED DETENTIONS FOLLOW-UP UNCHANGED — no new MoI announcements May 11-12; Iran has not commented publicly. No further Gulf-state internal action announced (no new UAE/Kuwait/Saudi arrests, no new airspace closures). UAE airspace restrictions imposed May 5 ended on/around May 11 (PRE-confirmed timeline). The May 9 arrest sweep remains the singular Gulf-internal action of the cycle; the 'second Gulf-state arrest sweep within 14 days' trigger from May 10 sits at Day 2 of 14, UNTRIPPED.** PRIOR FRAMING (preserved): Bahrain MoI announced May 9 arrest of 41 alleged IRGC-linked individuals — first major Gulf-state internal action against alleged IRGC network during cycle. Bahrain hosts US Fifth Fleet. The Gulf-state response to Iran continues openly bifurcated: Bahrain (pro-US, anti-IRGC), Saudi Arabia (deniable-pro-US-operationally, public-pro-mediation), UAE (covert third combatant per WSJ Lavan), Qatar (substantive mediator via IRGC contacts), Oman (silent mediator), Kuwait (denied airspace). **BAHRAIN MoI ANNOUNCED MAY 9 IT HAD ARRESTED 41 ALLEGED IRGC-LINKED INDIVIDUALS — FIRST MAJOR GULF-STATE INTERNAL ACTION AGAINST ALLEGED IRGC NETWORK DURING THE CYCLE.** Suspects had 'direct contact' with the IRGC and had collected funds intended to be sent to Iran. Investigations continue; further action anticipated (CONFIRMED — Khaleej Times primary, Washington Post, PBS NewsHour, Spectrum News, Anchorage Daily News, Inquirer/AP). Rights groups (HRW, others) argue Bahrain is using the war as cover to crack down on dissent generally — Bahrain hosts US Fifth Fleet and has historical Sunni-government / Shia-majority tensions where the regime has previously used 'Iran proxy' framing against Shia opposition (REPORTED — rights-group framing). **Two readings:** (a) substantive intelligence breakthrough — Bahrain has located IRGC funding/operational network and is dismantling it; (b) opportunistic crackdown using war as pretext — consistent with Bahrain's pattern. The truth may be both. Either way, this is a Gulf-state SIGNAL: Bahrain publicly aligning with the US blockade narrative against Iran, contrasting with Saudi Arabia's deniability-plus-Pakistan-alignment posture and Qatar's emergent IRGC-channel mediator role. **The Gulf-state response to Iran is now openly bifurcated:** Bahrain (pro-US, anti-IRGC), Saudi Arabia (deniable-pro-US-operationally, public-pro-mediation), UAE (continues hawkish per yesterday), Qatar (now openly mediating with IRGC contacts), Oman (silent mediator), Kuwait (denied airspace per WSJ scoop). PRIOR FRAMING (preserved): cumulative damage envelope from earlier cycle — 5th Fleet HQ, Bapco, ALBA, Batelco/AWS — still in effect.

active_triggers.condition: Second Gulf-state-led arrest sweep of alleged IRGC-affiliated network within 14 days (by 2026-05-24) following Bahrain's May 9 41-detentions — confirms US-led intel-sharing-and-disruption campaign against IRGC funding/operational architecture in Gulf
active_triggers.note_2026-06-03: RESOLVED untripped. The 14-day window (by 2026-05-24) closed with no second Gulf-state IRGC-network arrest sweep following Bahrain's May 9 detentions — the predicted US-led intel-sharing-and-disruption campaign against IRGC funding architecture did not materialise across the Gulf. Marked resolved on overdue-node refresh (node had been stale since 12 May).

recent_signals.headline: **BAHRAIN DIRECTLY TARGETED IN THE 3 JUNE BARRAGE — BDF INTERCEPTED 3 IRANIAN MISSILES AND A NUMBER OF DRONES AIMED AT 'CIVILIAN SITES,' NO CASUALTIES; IRGC FALSELY CLAIMED A FIFTH FLEET HQ HIT.** Bahrain's Defence Force General Command said it intercepted three Iranian missiles and several drones aimed at civilian sites, no casualties. The IRGC claimed it struck the US Fifth Fleet HQ in Manama; CENTCOM rejected the claim as FALSE (all attacks on US forces failed, no US assets harmed). Bahrain (Fifth Fleet host) was a direct interception party in the cycle's first casualty-producing barrage but absorbed the strikes cleanly — the lethal damage landed on Kuwait's civilian airport.
sources: Bahrain Defence Force General Command (official) — 3 missiles + drones intercepted, no casualties
CENTCOM via Gulf News / The National / RFE/RL — Fifth-Fleet-HQ claim FALSE, no US assets harmed
IRGC / Tasnim — claim of Fifth Fleet HQ hit (Iran claim)
recent_signals.verification: BDF interception of 3 missiles + drones, zero casualties = CONFIRMED ACTION; IRGC 'Fifth Fleet HQ hit' = CLAIMED, CENTCOM says FALSE (no weight).

recent_signals.headline: BAHRAIN MOI ANNOUNCED MAY 9 IT HAD ARRESTED 41 ALLEGED IRGC-LINKED INDIVIDUALS — FIRST MAJOR GULF-STATE INTERNAL ACTION AGAINST ALLEGED IRGC NETWORK DURING THE CYCLE. Suspects had 'direct contact' with IRGC and had collected funds intended to be sent to Iran; investigations continue, further action anticipated. Rights groups argue Bahrain may be using war as cover to crack down on dissent generally (Bahrain hosts US Fifth Fleet, historical Sunni-government / Shia-majority tensions). Two readings: (a) substantive intelligence breakthrough; (b) opportunistic crackdown. Either way, Bahrain is publicly aligning with US blockade narrative against Iran. Gulf-state response to Iran now openly bifurcated: Bahrain (pro-US, anti-IRGC), Saudi (deniable-pro-US, public-pro-mediation), UAE (hawkish), Qatar (mediating with IRGC contacts), Oman (silent), Kuwait (denied airspace).
recent_signals.verification: CONFIRMED ACTION — 41 arrests, public Bahrain MoI statement (Khaleej Times primary, Washington Post + PBS + AP wire pickup multi-source); Iran has NOT commented publicly; rights-group alternative framing = REPORTED (HRW, others)

historical_summaries.summary: 1 signals. Iranian strikes targeted US Navy 5th Fleet HQ in Manama and Crowne Plaza Hotel

3 signals. Bahrain's Bapco declared force majeure after waves of Iranian strikes targeted energy installations | Air raid sirens activated across Bahrain early Sunday March 29 | IRGC struck ALBA aluminum plant (world's largest smelter) — 2 mildly injured, damage assessed

9 signals. IRGC missiles hit Batelco telecommunications facility in Hamala, Bahrain on April 1 — the facility hosts Amazon Web Services infrastructure | IRGC struck Batelco telecommunications facility in Hamala, Bahrain on April 1 — hosts AWS cloud infrastructure, servers rendered 'partly ino | CONFIRMED: Bahrain's Bapco Energies oil storage tank hit by Iranian drone attack on April 5
